creator: marklmi26-fbsd@yahoo.com
text:
(In reply to Robert Clausecker from comment #24)

FYI: I've been making sure the Windows DevKit 2023 has only one USB drive,
the FreeBSD boot media, when I power it on for FreeBSD use (or reboot
FreeBSD). I power down before FreeBSD use. Part of the reason is all
FreeBSD the media I use with it are bootable. The built in UEFI/ACPI
does not do what other UEFI/ACPI's have done and provide a place that
lists media to select for booting. Seems to boot some sort of "first
found".

I turn on the other FreeBSD USB media (if any) after booting FreeBSD.

I did set the default to be USB media based booting, avoiding the need
to use any buttons to cause a USB media boot. If it does not find USB
media after a bit, it goes ahead and boots from the internal media
(Windows 11 Pro in my context).
